text.title: llamaindex-agents-eval Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for llamaindex-agents-eval. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use ReActAgent for agentic reasoning over query engines and custom tools. ReAct loops enable transparent reasoning before each action.
rule.rationale: ReAct provides interpretable reasoning traces; each "Thought," "Action," and "Observation" are logged for debugging.

rule.statement: Use OpenAIAgent for faster, cheaper reasoning via function calling. OpenAI agents delegate reasoning to the model's built-in function-calling logic.
rule.rationale: Function calling is faster and cheaper than explicit ReAct loops; trade transparency for latency and cost.

rule.statement: Add memory to agents using ChatMemoryBuffer. Agents then recall prior conversations within the token limit.
rule.rationale: Explicit memory (not part of the tools) enables multi-turn conversations with context recall.

rule.statement: Use MultiAgentRunner for orchestrating multiple specialized agents. Route queries to the appropriate agent based on the orchestrator prompt.
rule.rationale: Specialization improves performance; a research agent queries facts, an analysis agent runs calculations.

rule.statement: Use RetrieverEvaluator with metrics (hit_rate, MRR, NDCG) to measure retrieval quality. Generate synthetic Q&A pairs from documents using LLM then evaluate the retriever against them.
rule.rationale: Retrieval metrics reveal whether the right context is being surfaced. Hit rate (did top-K include correct document?) and MRR (mean reciprocal rank of first correct document) are standard IR metrics.

rule.statement: Use FaithfulnessEvaluator to measure whether responses are grounded in retrieved context. RelevancyEvaluator measures whether answers address the question. CorrectnessEvaluator compares against ground truth.
rule.rationale: These evaluators quantify hallucination, off-topic responses, and factual errors. Use them to detect degradation as the system evolves.

rule.statement: Use BatchEvalRunner for batch evaluation of multiple responses. Evaluators run in parallel; collect results and compute aggregate metrics.
rule.rationale: Batch evaluation scales to hundreds of queries. Aggregate metrics (mean score, pass rate) reveal system-wide quality trends.

rule.statement: Cache embedding operations to avoid recomputing embeddings for unchanged documents. Use EmbeddingCache or built-in caching in LlamaIndex.
rule.rationale: Embeddings are expensive; caching significantly reduces latency and cost for large document sets.

rule.statement: Use streaming to return responses token-by-token. LLMs typically support streaming; stream from the LLM, not just the retriever.
rule.rationale: Streaming reduces perceived latency — users see partial responses immediately instead of waiting for complete responses.

rule.statement: Use async retrieval (aget_relevant_documents or aquery) to retrieve from multiple sources in parallel. Await all retrievals before passing context to the LLM.
rule.rationale: Parallel async calls reduce latency. Example: query three vector stores and a keyword index simultaneously, then aggregate results.

rule.statement: Enable observability via LangSmith or LlamaIndex Observability. Log queries, retrieved context, LLM inputs/outputs, and evaluation metrics to a centralized trace store.
rule.rationale: Observability reveals bottlenecks, failure modes, and drift over time. Traces are essential for debugging production issues.

rule.statement: Use LlamaCloud for managed document parsing and indexing. Upload documents; LlamaCloud parses, chunks, and indexes them. Retrieve via the managed index API.
rule.rationale: Managed parsing handles complex document formats (PDFs, Office, HTML). Eliminates custom parsing code and its maintenance burden.

rule.statement: An orchestrating agent uses LlamaIndex agents as tools. Route queries to specialized agents (research, analysis, calculation) based on query intent.
rule.rationale: Specialization improves accuracy; a single generalist agent is less reliable than multiple focused agents.

rule.statement: Wrap LlamaIndex agent.chat() calls in error handling. Agents can fail to parse tool outputs or route incorrectly; catch and retry or escalate.
rule.rationale: Explicit error handling prevents silent failures. Retries with different prompts or models often succeed.

rule.statement: Log agent reasoning traces (Thought/Action/Observation from ReAct agents) for debugging and auditing. Use LangSmith or LlamaIndex Observability to centralize logs.
rule.rationale: Reasoning traces reveal where agents make mistakes and enable iterative prompt refinement.

rule.statement: Cache embedding results to avoid recomputing embeddings for identical or similar documents. Use LlamaIndex's built-in embedding cache or Redis backend.
rule.rationale: Embeddings are expensive (API calls, latency). Caching reduces both cost and latency for repeated queries over the same documents.

rule.statement: Stream token-level output from LLM responses. Use query_engine.query(..., stream=True) or agent.stream_chat() where available.
rule.rationale: Token-level streaming reduces perceived latency. Users see partial responses immediately instead of waiting for complete generation.

rule.statement: Convert retrieval to async (aget_relevant_documents, aquery) to parallelize multiple document lookups. Retrieve from vector store, keyword index, and database concurrently, then aggregate.
rule.rationale: Parallel async retrieval significantly reduces latency. Requires proper error handling for partial failures.

rule.statement: Configure LangSmith observability: set LANGSMITH_API_KEY and LANGSMITH_PROJECT to trace every agent.chat() call, every tool invocation, and every evaluation run.
rule.rationale: Centralized observability reveals performance bottlenecks, error patterns, and drift in evaluation metrics. Essential for production debugging.

rule.statement: Use LlamaCloud for document ingestion. Upload documents; LlamaCloud handles parsing, chunking, and metadata extraction. Query via managed indices.
rule.rationale: Managed parsing supports complex formats (PDFs, Word, HTML, images with OCR). Eliminates custom parsing code and its maintenance.

rule.statement: Evaluation dataset quality directly impacts metric reliability. Use LLM to generate 2-5 Q&A pairs per document chunk. Validate a sample manually before running full evaluation.
rule.rationale: Low-quality synthetic Q&A pairs produce misleading metrics. Manual validation catches generation errors early.

rule.statement: For iterative improvement, track evaluation metrics over time. Store results (hit rate, faithfulness, relevancy) in a database. Monitor for degradation.
rule.rationale: Metrics trending downward indicate the system is degrading as documents are added or prompts change. Early detection enables rapid fixes.

rule.statement: Batch evaluation saves costs and enables parallel metric computation. Prepare 50-200 test questions; run BatchEvalRunner with multiple evaluators (faithfulness, relevancy, correctness); aggregate results.
rule.rationale: Batch evaluation reduces per-query overhead and enables statistical analysis of system behavior. Aggregate metrics reveal system-wide quality trends, not just individual response quality.

rule.statement: When evaluation shows low hit_rate, the retriever is missing relevant documents. Investigate: improve chunk size, add more documents, or re-train embeddings. When faithfulness is low, the LLM is hallucinating; use stronger models or better prompts.
rule.rationale: Each metric pinpoints a specific failure mode. Root cause analysis enables targeted fixes rather than blind system tuning.

rule.statement: Never skip manual validation of synthetic Q&A datasets. Review a random sample of generated questions; if more than 10% are low-quality (ambiguous, unanswerable, off-topic), regenerate with a stronger LLM.
rule.rationale: Garbage input produces garbage metrics. Manual spot-checks catch generation issues before wasting time on evaluation runs.

rule.statement: For production, enable LangSmith tracing on every agent.chat() call. Set LANGSMITH_API_KEY and LANGSMITH_PROJECT environment variables. Traces should include: query, tool calls, tool outputs, LLM inputs, LLM outputs, final response.
rule.rationale: Production systems require observability. Traces enable rapid debugging of agent failures and performance analysis.

rule.statement: Use MemorySaver for development only. For production, use PostgreSQL (AsyncPostgresSaver) or Redis checkpointing so agent conversations survive process restarts and can be resumed.
rule.rationale: In-process memory is lost on crashes. Durable storage enables reliable agent state persistence.

rule.statement: Test agents with adversarial queries: questions outside the domain, contradictory statements, requests for harmful information. Agents should gracefully acknowledge limits rather than hallucinate answers.
rule.rationale: Agents often fail gracefully under distribution shift. Adversarial testing reveals these failure modes before production deployment.

rule.statement: Version your agents and evaluation datasets. Tag semantic versions (e.g., agent-v1.2.0, eval-dataset-v2.1.0). Tie metric improvements to specific agent/dataset versions for reproducibility and rollback.
rule.rationale: Version control enables A/B testing and rollback on regressions. Without versions, it's impossible to correlate metric changes to specific changes.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'r-advanced' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'r-default-high' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'r-default-low' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.
